you.d: the person I am talking to

years.d: A long period of time, like 365 days

year.e: My birthday is in the same year as my dog's.

yet.d: still or in addition to what has happened

yes.d: Saying you agree or will do something
yes.e: I said yes when my mom asked me to clean my room.

yeah.d: A word used to show agreement or excitement
yeah.e: When I told my friend I got a new bike, he said, 'Yeah, that's so cool!'

york.d: a state in the northeastern United States
york.e: New York City is a very big and busy city.

yourself.e: You should be proud of yourself for trying your best.

yesterday.e: I played soccer with my friends yesterday.

youth.d: The time of life when you are young
youth.e: The youth group at my church goes on fun trips.

yours.e: That bike is yours, not mine.

ya.d: a way to say 'yes' in a friendly way
ya.e: Ya, I want to play with you.

younger.d: Being less old than someone or something else
younger.e: My little sister is younger than me.

yellow.d: A bright color like the sun or a lemon.
yellow.e: The sun shone a bright yellow light on the flowers.

youtube.d: A website where people can watch and share videos online.
youtube.e: I like watching funny videos on YouTube.

yard.d: A piece of land around a house
yard.e: I like to play in the yard with my dog.
